name: Upside Down Apple Tart
recipeInstructions:
1. Spread 2 tablespoons of the butter in a 9-inch deep pie plate; arrange lemon slices in the bottom; sprinkle with 1 tablespoon of the sugar.
2. Pare, quarter and core apples; slice into 1/2-inch thick wedges. Arrange in layers in prepared pie plate, sprinkling 2 more tablespoons sugar between layers.
3. Heat remaining sugar in a small heavy skillet until it melts and becomes a light brown syrup. Remove from heat; add remaining butter, stirring until melted; pour over apples.
4. Prepare piecrust mix, following label directions, or make pastry from your own single-crust recipe. Roll out to a 10-inch round on lightly floured surface; place over apples. (Do not attach pastry to edge.)
5. Bake at 425°F for 30 minutes or until crust is golden brown. Cool on wire rack 15 minutes or until ready to serve. Invert onto shallow serving dish. Serve slightly warm with whipped cream.
